evidence_excerpt: At discharge the patient remained chronically confused and lethargic with dependence for mobility, and was sent to an extended care facility without documented resolution of the primary condition.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Discharge Mental Status: Confused - always; Level of Consciousness: Lethargic but arousable.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Upon arrival to the floor, patient was more calm; more responsive and redirectable when daughter was present.
Psychotropic regimen modified at discharge (e.g., chlorpromazine scheduled and PRN, divalproex added, haloperidol IM PRN, risperidone ODT).

notes_on_uncertainty: No follow-up beyond this hospitalization is provided, so long-term outcome is inferred from discharge status. The chief complaint mentions 'admission to inpatient hospice,' but the discharge disposition was to an extended care facility; hospice enrollment is not corroborated. Timing of initial ED agitation and soft restraints may predate the chosen index time.
